title: HealthMate
overview: HealthMate is a telehealth platform that simplifies patient-provider communication. Patients can book appointments, review records, and message their care team — all in one accessible, HIPAA-compliant app.
challenge: Patients — especially older adults and those managing chronic conditions — found existing telehealth apps confusing and anxiety-inducing. The interface felt clinical and cold, and key features were buried under layers of navigation.
solution: We redesigned the entire patient journey around empathy and clarity. Large tap targets, plain-language copy, and a calm colour system made HealthMate feel approachable. A new unified inbox reduced the #1 support complaint overnight.
research: Contextual interviews with 18 patients across age groups 35–75
Accessibility audit against WCAG 2.1 AA standards
Journey mapping workshops with clinical staff
A/B tested 4 navigation patterns with 300-participant panel

process.description: Full WCAG 2.1 AA audit revealed 34 critical failures. These became the first design sprint's backlog.
Synthesised patient interviews into three key personas: the Anxious Patient, the Busy Carer, and the Digital Newcomer.
Built an accessible design system with 4.5:1 minimum contrast ratios, 44px minimum tap targets, and screen-reader-tested components.
Final prototype achieved 96% task success with older-adult participants — up from 61% at project start.

results: Patient satisfaction score increased from 3.2 to 4.7 / 5
Support tickets down 44% in the first quarter post-launch
WCAG 2.1 AA certified — the first telehealth app in its category
